steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context / vocabulary question. The frame “It is by no means ______” negates whatever word fills the blank, and “indeed, Farhadi himself cites Bruegel as an inspiration” confirms that recognizing Bruegel’s influence is clearly and genuinely warranted. The blank needs a word that, when negated by “by no means,” produces the meaning “definitely worth recognizing.”

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "substantial": “By no means substantial” means the recognition is not meaningful or weighty — the opposite of what the passage supports. ✗
  • "satisfying": “By no means satisfying” means the recognition is not pleasing — irrelevant to whether the influence is real, and contradicted by Farhadi’s own confirmation. ✗
  • "unimportant": “By no means unimportant” means the recognition is definitely important — consistent with the artist himself citing Bruegel and with “indeed” reinforcing the certainty. ✓
  • "appropriate": “By no means appropriate” means the recognition is not proper or suitable — again the opposite of what the passage establishes. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
